summary.body: Computer Engineering undergraduate at UFS with experience in full-stack development, test automation, CI/CD, and applied research. I build software with a focus on maintainability, delivery quality, and measurable impact, combining product validation with engineering practices.

bullet_points: Built a React Native (Expo) and Spring Boot mobile MVP for pilot launch, delivering five core flows (authentication, campaign creation, donations, donation history, and voucher creation/recovery) and 63 end-to-end regression scenarios for those flows using Maestro for UI automation and WireMock for API mocking, improving release stability and troubleshooting efficiency.
Eliminated a single point of failure in the payment flow by designing a multi-provider failover system, increasing availability from 0% to ~99% during provider outages.
Reduced deployment time by 75%, from 1 hour to 15 minutes, saving ~1.5 SWE/h per week across 2 weekly releases, by implementing CI/CD with GitHub Actions and Terraform-managed AWS infrastructure.

Built a plagiarism-detection benchmark dataset by scraping Codeforces problems and solutions via a custom Python pipeline with parsing and retry logic, addressing the lack of reliable validation data in the literature, and assembling 500 solutions to distinct problems and generating obfuscated variants.

Implemented a Python-based Tree-sitter converter to generate reversible syntax-tree JSON representations for automated code comparison, achieving same-language plagiarism-detection F1-scores of 86.36% for Python and 68.63% for C with the dataset.

Won 2nd place at the ChipLabs Hackathon for building an LLM routing module for multiple AI APIs using LangChain and PostgreSQL as a vector database, reducing inference cost by routing prompts to the smallest adequate model instead of a fixed default.

Achieved 63rd place out of 984 teams in the SBC 2025 Programming Marathon by coaching the team through structured problem-solving sessions, contest simulations, and targeted practice on algorithmic weaknesses.
